segments.text: Business policies live in prose, but customer decisions live in code. One strict inequality or misplaced exception can produce the wrong refund. PolicyTwin makes those gaps visible, testable, and reviewable.
During Build Week, Codex with GPT-5.6 mapped the repository, shaped strict PolicyIR, built the product, added adversarial tests, and verified review fixes. The primary feedback session is included.

Decision Queue makes material uncertainty explicit instead of hiding it in a prompt. It versions purchase-day counting, the usage measurement point, and the default outcome, while a contradictory authoritative example is rejected before policy verification can continue.

Deterministic TypeScript compiles the accepted model to Rego, and checksum-pinned OPA passes all 41 accepted cases. Golden, boundary, conflict, and minimal-contrast cases exercise every rule. A separate reference mutation run kills 44 of 47 plausible defects.

In the bounded local challenge run, Codex authored replacements for exactly two files. PolicyTwin derived the filesystem diff, passed seven of seven regression tests, replayed all 41 accepted policy cases with zero drift, and required a separate read-only Codex review that returned approve with no blockers. The hosted production repair control stays visibly blocked because its stronger isolation is unavailable.

Proof keeps every claim attached to machine-readable evidence: PolicyIR, Rego, OPA results, drift witnesses, mutation results, traceability, and security reports. The archive is deterministic and hash-covered, while unavailable production claims remain explicitly marked as not run.

A proposed change from 14 to 30 days reveals three affected rules, eight changed cases, one golden conflict, and five code locations before any new repair. The accepted version and its existing proof remain unchanged.
This is local challenge evidence, not production attestation. Judges can reproduce the seeded flow, inspect the two-file diff, and validate the 41-case result directly from the public repository.
